description.value:
A wax-sealed packet bearing High King Harald's personal cipher — not the royal seal, but a private mark known only to his most trusted agents. The parchment inside is written in a substitution code whose meaning depends on which key phrase the reader knows.

Effect (once, as a maneuver): Break the seal and present the cipher to any crown-loyal NPC (Chainwarden officer of rank Sergeant+, settlement leader, or sworn crown agent). That creature must make a medium Intuition test to authenticate it. On tier 2+, for the remainder of this scene the bearer gains an edge on Presence tests with crown-loyal NPCs, passes Chainwarden checkpoints without inspection or toll, and may requisition up to 2 days of cold-weather rations and 1 piece of standard equipment from any crown supply post.

Warning: Single-use. Once the seal is broken, the parchment degrades within the hour. Harald's seal cannot be faked a second time.
description.director:
Received: Beat 3 — Harald gives this with forged papers and rations. His exact words: 'Trust no one in my name. If someone quotes my orders, assume they're lying or compromised. Use this only in dire need.'

Optimal uses: Beat 4a — Greypass Toll negotiation (grants an edge on Presence tests, lets them pass without toll). Beat 9 — signalling wall guards during the siege breach (major advantage in the final montage).

Grafvitnir pressure: If used in the presence of a Pale Maw agent or Grafvitnir operative (which includes Dreyfus or any Wyrd-touched NPC), the cipher's use is reported through the root-whisper network. Increase the Grafvitnir Pressure Check by +2.
